content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 25, 1986


Hon. David G. Sils
Orange County Superior Court
P. O. Box 1994
Santa Ana, CA  92702-1994

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-86-184

Dear Judge Sils:

     Thank you for your letter of May 21, 1986, confirming my
telephone advice of May 20, 1986, with regard to your filing
obligations under the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015).

     Your letter correctly summarizes my advice that a candidate
who has no opposition and will, therefore, not be listed on the
ballot is not required to file pre-election campaign statements
provided that no contributions have been received and no expendi-
tures have been made during the period covered by the pre-election
statement. You have stated that contributions were received and
expenditures made during the period January 1, 1986 through
March 17, 1986, and that you filed a report covering that period.
During the period March 18, 1986 through May 17, 1986, you
received no contributions and made no expenditures. Therefore,
your next campaign disclosure statement is due no later than
July 31, 1986, covering the period March 18, 1986 through
June 30, 1986.

     Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662
if you have further questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Carla Wardlow
                                        Political Reform Consultant


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Superior Court of the State of California
County of Orange

700 CIVIC CENTER DRIVE WEST                                MAY 27 10 24 AM '86
P.O. BOX 1994

Chambers of                    Santa Ana, California 92702-1994
HON. DAVID G. SILLS
Judge of Superior Court                                    (714) 834-3734


May 21, 1986


Fair Political Practices Committee
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Attention:  Carla Wordlow

Dear Ms. Wordlow:

     This will confirm my conversation with you yesterday
morning at which time I advised you that although I am a
candidate for re-election, I have no opposition.  As a
result, my name will not even appear on the ballot -- an
election is deemed automatic for trial court judges.  In
addition, I advised you that although I have raised funds in
the past, no funds have been raised nor expenditures made
since the closing date of the last reporting period.  Reports
were filed for that period.

     Under such circumstances, you have informed me that
since there is no election for my position, pre-election
disclosure reports required of candidates not later than May
22, 1986 are not required in my case.  The next reports
required are the semiannual reports for the period ending
June 30, 1986.

     Thank you for your courtesies and cooperation.

                                   Very truly yours,


                                   [signature]

                                   DAVID G. SILLS
                                   Judge of Superior Court

DGS:go

sections.conclusion: Your next campaign disclosure statement is due no later than July 31, 1986, covering the period March 18, 1986 through June 30, 1986.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations for a judicial candidate who has no opposition and will not appear on the ballot, and when is the next required filing?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A candidate with no opposition and no ballot listing is not required to file pre-election campaign statements if no contributions or expenditures occurred during that period. The next required filing is a semiannual report due July 31, 1986, covering the period with no campaign activity.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (Judge Sills, May 21, 1986) and FPPC response letter (Carla Wardlow, June 25, 1986). No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers, but content is clearly delineated. Date discrepancy noted: May 20 phone advice referenced in response letter but incoming request dated May 21.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations for a judicial candidate who has no opposition and will not appear on the ballot, and when is the next required filing?

CONCLUSION: Your next campaign disclosure statement is due no later than July 31, 1986, covering the period March 18, 1986 through June 30, 1986.

embedding.summary: FPPC responded to Judge David G. Sills' inquiry confirming that judicial candidates facing no opposition and not appearing on the ballot are exempt from pre-election filing requirements when no contributions or expenditures occur during the relevant period. The next required filing is a semiannual campaign disclosure statement due July 31, 1986.
